name: Babur's Garden
description: Mughal emperor Babur's 16th-century terraced garden – one of the finest in the Islamic world, recently restored.

name: National Museum of Afghanistan
description: Holds some of Afghanistan's greatest treasures including Gandharan Buddhist art and Bactrian gold.

name: Band-e Amir Lakes
description: Six strikingly blue crater lakes held by natural travertine dams at 3,000 m altitude – Afghanistan's first national park.

name: Bamiyan Buddha Niches
description: The colossal niches that once held the world's tallest standing Buddhas, carved in the 6th century CE.

name: Herat Citadel (Qala Ikhtiyaruddin)
description: An ancient citadel rebuilt by Alexander the Great, later beautified by Timurid rulers, now a major museum.

name: Friday Mosque (Masjid-i-Jami)
description: One of the finest examples of Islamic architecture in Central Asia, adorned with exquisite tilework from the 12th century.

name: Shrine of Hazrat Ali (Blue Mosque)
description: Afghanistan's most famous building – a breathtaking blue-tiled mosque and shrine, especially magnificent during Nawruz.

name: Ancient City of Balkh
description: One of the world's oldest cities, the 'Mother of Cities', with ruins spanning Zoroastrian, Greek and Islamic periods.

name: Shahr-e Gholghola (City of Screams)
description: Haunting ruins of the pre-Islamic capital of Bamiyan, destroyed by Genghis Khan in the 13th century.

name: Chicken Street Bazaar
description: Kabul's famous street market for carpets, lapis lazuli, antiques, and Afghan handicrafts.

name: Darul Aman Palace
description: A grand neo-classical palace from the 1920s with a striking silhouette – under restoration.

name: Gawhar Shad Mausoleum
description: The jewel of the Musalla Complex – the elaborate tomb of Queen Gawhar Shad, a great Timurid patron.
